model_routing.description: Strategic model selection based on task complexity and quality requirements
model_routing.philosophy: Right model for right task (not cheapest model everywhere)

rules.reasoning: Heartbeats just check if action is needed. Nano is 95% cheaper than Sonnet.
rules.example_prompt: Read HEARTBEAT.md if it exists. If nothing needs attention, reply HEARTBEAT_OK.

rules.reasoning: No ambiguity, no strategic thinking, correct or incorrect.
examples: Fetch latest posts from WordPress API
Fill in email template with variables

rules.reasoning: Fast, cost-effective, good quality for casual chat.
rules.when_to_upgrade_to_sonnet: Complex explanations, code generation, sensitive topics

rules.quality_threshold: Very High (personal tone, founder voice, nuanced)
rules.why_not_haiku: Generic responses destroy trust (see Case 5: Why Haiku Failed)

rules.reasoning: Public-facing content with founder's name. Quality non-negotiable.
rules.quality_threshold: Very High (founder voice, storytelling, no AI patterns)
rules.why_not_haiku: Reads like content mill output (40-50% quality drop)
rules.why_not_mistral: Good but lacks consistent founder voice tone

rules.reasoning: Complex analysis, business decisions, multi-factor prioritization.
rules.when_to_use: Crons that analyze/prioritize, infrequent high-stakes decisions

rules.quality_threshold: High (root cause analysis, not surface fixes)
rules.why_not_haiku: Suggests generic fixes, doesn't read docs deeply
rules.override_to_opus: Critical production outage, complex distributed system

rules.reasoning: Code quality matters. Bugs = time wasted debugging.
rules.when_haiku_ok: Trivial edits (fix typo, update config value)

rules.reasoning: Sub-agents need quality output but focused context reduces token cost.
examples: Growth Hacker: ICE scoring, funnel analysis
Campaign Builder: email sequences, social copy
UI Designer: Lighthouse audits, accessibility reviews
rules.why_not_haiku: Domain reasoning requires Sonnet minimum — tested and confirmed
rules.why_not_opus: Sub-agents have focused context (5KB); don't need full orchestration depth

rules.reasoning: Sonnet is the best cost/quality orchestrator (8.5/10, $0.015/call). Upgrade to Opus for 3+ agents in parallel or strategic decisions (9.5/10, $0.075/call). Haiku CANNOT synthesize — it routes only.
examples: Decompose complex request into sub-agent tasks
rules.why_not_sonnet: Orchestration decisions affect entire workflow — needs deepest reasoning
rules.pattern: Spawn parallel sub-agents → wait for completion → synthesize → present to human

all_providers_tested.verdict: Best synthesis; use for 3+ agents or strategic
Solid but pricier than Sonnet for same quality
Budget option; decent synthesis, occasional shallow outputs
2-agent sequential only; excellent daily default but weak multi-agent synthesis
Budget; fails on complex dependency chains
NOT RECOMMENDED — routes only, cannot synthesize
Speed-only — too shallow for orchestration decisions

benchmark.key_insight: Orchestration != Execution. Cheap models route. Only capable models synthesize. The $0.01/call difference between Haiku and Sonnet = 50% quality loss. Mistral Large is an excellent daily default — a distinct role from orchestrator.

monthly_cost_20x.haiku: $0.95 (NOT recommended — worse quality for similar price)

rules.upgrade_trigger: 3+ parallel agents OR strategic decisions (investment, product direction, go/no-go)
rules.why_not_haiku: Quality 4/10. Routes tasks but cannot synthesize. Output: paste of agent reports. Not acceptable for production orchestration.

decision_tree.question_2: Is there a quality threshold? (e.g., founder voice, public-facing)
decision_tree.question_3: Does it involve relationships or editorial content?
decision_tree.question_4: Is it strategic thinking or complex analysis?

blanket_downgrade.mistake: Use Haiku for everything to save money
blanket_downgrade.better: Use routing rules based on actual task analysis

one_size_fits_all.better: Override model per task type (crons, subagents)

optimistic_savings.mistake: Assume 80% tasks can use Haiku → $600/year savings
optimistic_savings.reality: Only 25-33% tasks viable with Haiku → $180/year realistic
optimistic_savings.better: Measure actual tasks, calculate realistic savings

ignoring_time_cost.mistake: Use cheap model, spend 30 min fixing output
ignoring_time_cost.better: Use right model upfront, save editing time

metrics_to_track: Quality degradation rate (how often you reject AI output)
Model distribution (% Nano, Haiku, Mistral, Sonnet, Opus)
recommended_tools: Monthly cost review (compare actual vs projected)
red_flags: Spending >50% of budget on heartbeats (switch to Nano)
High rejection rate on Haiku tasks (upgrade to Sonnet)
Strategic crons using Haiku (upgrade to Opus)
Editorial content using Mistral (upgrade to Sonnet)

faq.q1: Can I use GPT-4o instead of Sonnet?
faq.a1: Yes, but test quality first. GPT-4o is good for code, less strong for founder voice tone.
faq.a2: Gemini Flash is viable alternative to Mistral Large. Gemini Pro competes with Sonnet.
faq.q3: Should I upgrade to Opus for all editorial?
faq.a3: No. Sonnet handles 90% of editorial well. Opus for flagship content only (book, major launches).
faq.q4: How often should I review routing rules?
faq.a4: Monthly cost review. Adjust if seeing quality issues or cost spikes.
faq.a5: Partially. Use cron model overrides. For dynamic routing, need custom logic (planned feature).
